finding.contract_claim: React's Rules of Hooks require every hook to be called unconditionally, in the same order, on every render of a component.
finding.observed_behavior: OnboardingPage calls useState at lines 8-9 after two conditional early returns (loading at line 5, hasOrgs at line 6), so the hooks are skipped on some renders and hook state is corrupted once the conditions change between renders.
finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: React's Rules of Hooks require all hooks to be called unconditionally and in the same order on every render. Lines 5-6 return early when `loading` or `hasOrgs` is true, meaning `useState` on lines 8-9 is never reached on those renders. When the component transitions — e.g., `loading` flips from `true` to `false` — React's hook order bookkeeping is violated, causing the hook call count to change between renders, leading to hook state corruption or the 'Rendered more hooks than during the previous render' runtime error. Significance: This is a real React bug that can corrupt component state on state transitions. Actionability: Move all `useState` calls to before the conditional early returns (lines 5-6).
finding.suggested_fix: Move `const [name, setName] = useState("")` and `const [slug, setSlug] = useState("")` to before the conditional early returns on lines 5-6, so all hooks are called unconditionally on every render of OnboardingPage.
